weather.description: Warm and pleasant with occasional showers

quickFacts: More bicycles than people — over 880,000 bikes in the city
Built on 11 million wooden poles driven into marshy ground
Home to more than 165 canals totalling over 100 km

places.name: Jordaan
places.caption: Amsterdam's most charming neighborhood
places.description: Once a working-class area, the Jordaan is now one of Amsterdam's most desirable neighborhoods. Wander its narrow streets lined with indie boutiques, cozy brown cafés, and hidden courtyard gardens called hofjes.

places.name: Anne Frank House
places.caption: A powerful and moving piece of history
places.description: The actual hiding place where Anne Frank wrote her famous diary during WWII. The museum preserves the secret annex behind a bookcase and offers an emotional, deeply human experience. Book tickets well in advance.

places.name: Vondelpark
places.caption: Amsterdam's beloved green lung
places.description: This sprawling 47-hectare park is where locals come to picnic, rollerblade, and catch free open-air concerts in summer. Grab a terrace seat at 't Blauwe Theehuis, the flying-saucer-shaped café in the center.

places.name: Rijksmuseum
places.caption: Dutch Golden Age masterpieces
places.description: Home to Rembrandt's Night Watch and Vermeer's Milkmaid, this grand museum houses 8,000 objects spanning 800 years of Dutch art. The building itself — a neo-Gothic masterpiece — is worth the visit alone.

places.name: De Pijp
places.caption: Multicultural vibes and the best street food
places.description: Amsterdam's most diverse neighborhood revolves around the Albert Cuyp Market, where you can grab fresh stroopwafels, Surinamese roti, and Dutch herring. The surrounding streets buzz with international restaurants and quirky bars.

places.name: NDSM Wharf
places.caption: Post-industrial creative playground
places.description: A former shipyard across the IJ river transformed into Amsterdam's edgiest cultural hub. Giant street art, underground festivals, and the monthly IJ-Hallen flea market draw creative crowds. Take the free ferry from Centraal.

places.name: Nine Streets (De Negen Straatjes)
places.caption: Boutique shopping in canal-side charm
places.description: Nine tiny streets connecting the main canals, packed with independent shops, vintage stores, and specialty cheese and chocolate shops. It's the perfect antidote to generic high-street shopping.

places.name: A'DAM Lookout
places.caption: Swing over the edge of Amsterdam
places.description: A rooftop observation deck on top of the A'DAM Tower in Amsterdam-Noord with panoramic city views. The real draw is the Over the Edge swing — Europe's highest swing — dangling you 100 meters above the ground.

places.name: Begijnhof
places.caption: A secret medieval courtyard
places.description: Tucked behind an unmarked door off the Spui square, this tranquil 14th-century courtyard was once home to a community of religious women. It includes one of Amsterdam's two remaining wooden houses and a hidden chapel.

places.name: Brouwersgracht
places.caption: Amsterdam's most beautiful canal
places.description: Often called the most picturesque canal in the city, Brouwersgracht is lined with converted warehouse houseboats and golden-age merchant houses. Walk it at sunset when the gabled facades glow amber.

places.name: Jordaan District
places.caption: Charming canal neighbourhood
places.description: Narrow streets lined with indie boutiques, brown cafés and galleries. The Nine Streets (De Negen Straatjes) are perfect for wandering.

places.name: Albert Cuyp Market
places.caption: Street market since 1905
places.description: Amsterdam's largest and most popular street market. Fresh stroopwafels, Dutch cheese, herring, and vintage finds.

places.name: Heineken Experience
places.caption: Beer history & tastings
places.description: Interactive brewery tour in the original 1867 Heineken brewery. Ends with a tasting on the rooftop terrace.

places.name: Museumplein
places.caption: Culture square
places.description: Open square flanked by the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum and Stedelijk. The I Amsterdam sign photo spot (when it's there).

places.name: Canal Ring Cruise
places.caption: See the city from water
places.description: UNESCO-listed 17th-century canal ring. Evening cruises with wine are magical — 165 canals, 1,500 bridges.

foodScene: Amsterdam's food scene is way more than just cheese and pancakes. Hit up Foodhallen in Oud-West for gourmet street food under one roof — try the bitterballen at De Ballenbar and Vietnamese bánh mì at Viet View. For classic Dutch, order stamppot (mashed potato with kale and smoked sausage) at Moeders, a restaurant whose walls are covered in photos of guests' mothers. The Surinamese-Javanese roti at Warung Spang Makandra in De Pijp is legendary. Don't skip fresh herring from a haringhandel (herring cart) topped with onions and pickles — Frens Haringhandel near the Koningsplein bridge is a local favorite. For brunch, The Breakfast Club and Pluk serve Instagram-worthy stacks. Wash it all down with local craft beer from Brouwerij 't IJ, brewed in a windmill.
